sections.heading: 1. Introduction
content: This document outlines the rules, guidelines, and behavioral expectations for all individuals and organizations that interact with Adymire Agency ("the Company," "We," or "Us").
• Any party engaging with the Company in any capacity
By interacting with the Company, you agree to abide by these Rules & Guidelines. Violation of these rules may result in termination of services, project cancellation, or legal action.

sections.heading: 2. Purpose of these guidelines
bullets: Establish clear behavioral expectations
Maintain a professional and respectful working environment
Protect the rights and interests of both the Company and clients
Ensure smooth project execution and collaboration
Define acceptable and unacceptable conduct
Provide a framework for conflict resolution

sections.heading: 3. Core principles
content: All interactions with Adymire Agency must be guided by these core principles:

subSections.heading: 3.1 Professionalism
bullets: Maintain courteous and professional communication at all times
Respect the expertise and time of Company staff
Conduct business in a transparent and ethical manner

subSections.heading: 3.2 Honesty and integrity
bullets: Provide truthful information in all communications
Disclose relevant facts that may impact project execution
Do not engage in fraudulent or deceptive practices

subSections.heading: 3.3 Respect and courtesy
bullets: Treat all Company personnel with dignity and respect
Avoid abusive, threatening, or discriminatory language
Recognize that staff are professionals deserving of courteous treatment

subSections.heading: 3.4 Collaboration & 3.5 Accountability
bullets: Work cooperatively with the Company team
Provide timely feedback and approvals
Honor commitments and take responsibility for your role in project success

sections.heading: 4.1 Official communication channels
content: • Phone calls (for urgent matters or scheduled discussions)
• WhatsApp (for quick updates, subject to Company approval)
• Personal social media accounts of staff members
• Contact outside of business hours unless emergency

sections.heading: 4.2 Communication etiquette
content: • Respond to Company communications within reasonable timeframes (24-48 hours for standard queries)
• Use professional subject lines and greetings
• Keep conversations focused on the project or topic
• Use offensive, abusive, or threatening language
• Contact staff during holidays or outside business hours for non-urgent matters

sections.heading: 4.3 Response time expectations
bullets: Standard inquiries: 24-48 business hours
Urgent matters: Same business day (if communicated during business hours)
Complex questions requiring research: 3-5 business days
Weekends and holidays: Next business day

sections.heading: 4.4 Meeting protocol
bullets: Meetings must be scheduled in advance
Be punctual for all scheduled meetings
Come prepared with relevant information and questions
Designate appropriate decision-makers to attend
If unable to attend, provide at least 24 hours' notice
Respect the scheduled duration

subSections.heading: 5.1 Client responsibilities
content: • Provide complete and accurate project requirements at the outset
• Submit all required assets, content, and resources on time
• Notify the Company immediately if requirements change
• Review deliverables within the agreed timeframe (typically 3-7 business days)
• Avoid vague comments like 'make it better' or 'I don't like it'
• Consolidate feedback from multiple stakeholders before submitting
• Understand that excessive or contradictory feedback may delay projects
• Designate a primary point of contact with decision-making authority
• Be available for scheduled calls, meetings, and reviews
• Respond to queries and approval requests promptly
• Inform the Company of planned absences or unavailability
• Understand that changes outside the original scope require additional time and budget
• Submit change requests formally and in writing
• Accept that the Company has final say on feasibility of changes

subSections.heading: 5.2 Revision policy
bullets: Number of revisions specified in agreement
Revisions must be based on original approved scope
New requirements do not constitute revisions
Additional revisions beyond limit incur extra charges
Revisions must be requested within specified timeframe

subSections.heading: 5.3 Approval and sign-off
bullets: Clients must formally approve phases before moving forward
Silence/non-response within the approval period (typically 7 days) is considered acceptance
Once approved, changes require additional charges

subSections.heading: 5.4 Testing and quality assurance
content: • Company conducts internal testing before delivery
• Clients are responsible for user acceptance testing
• Bug reports must be submitted in a structured format with:
• Bugs arising from client-requested changes or customizations may be billed separately

subSections.heading: 6.1 Payment obligations
bullets: Invoices must be paid according to agreed terms
Late payments may incur interest charges and service suspension
Clients must notify the Company immediately of any payment issues
Disputes regarding invoices must be raised within 7 days of receipt

subSections.heading: 6.2 Prohibited financial conduct
bullets: Request work to commence without making required advance payments
Delay payments with the intention of leveraging completed work
Initiate chargebacks without first attempting to resolve issues with the Company
Provide false information to avoid payment obligations
Request refunds based on false or exaggerated claims

subSections.heading: 6.3 Pricing discussions
bullets: Pricing is determined by the Company based on project complexity and scope
Attempting to negotiate pricing through aggressive or manipulative tactics is not acceptable
The Company's pricing decisions are final unless mutually agreed changes occur
Comparing pricing with competitors or making unrealistic demands is discouraged

subSections.heading: 7.1 Client obligations
bullets: Claim ownership of the Company's proprietary tools, methods, or processes
Reverse-engineer Company software or platforms
Copy or reproduce Company materials without authorization
Share source code or proprietary information with third parties
Use Company branding, logos, or identity without permission

subSections.heading: 7.2 Proper attribution
bullets: When showcasing work created by the Company, provide appropriate credit
Do not present Company work as solely your own creation
Respect confidentiality agreements regarding Company methods

subSections.heading: 8.1 Client obligations
bullets: Protect access credentials and login information
Do not share project files or Company deliverables with unauthorized parties
Maintain confidentiality of Company pricing, methods, and business practices
Report any security breaches or data leaks immediately

subSections.heading: 8.2 Company obligations
bullets: Protect client confidential information
Limit access to client data to authorized personnel only
Implement reasonable security measures
Notify clients promptly of any security incidents

subSections.heading: 9.1 Abusive behavior
bullets: Verbal abuse, threats, or harassment toward Company staff
Use of profanity, derogatory language, or personal attacks
Intimidation tactics or threatening legal action without cause
Discrimination based on race, gender, religion, nationality, or other protected characteristics

subSections.heading: 9.2 Dishonest practices
bullets: Providing false information or fraudulent documents
Misrepresenting qualifications, budget, or project requirements
Attempting to manipulate or deceive Company staff
Identity fraud or impersonation

subSections.heading: 9.3 Misuse of services
bullets: Using Company services for illegal activities
Requesting creation of content that violates laws or ethical standards
Distributing malware, viruses, or harmful code
Attempting to hack, disrupt, or compromise Company systems

subSections.heading: 9.4 Unfair business practices
bullets: Attempting to hire Company staff directly during or immediately after a project (poaching)
Soliciting Company subcontractors or partners without consent
Publicly defaming or disparaging the Company based on false information
Manipulation tactics such as threatening negative reviews to obtain refunds or free work

subSections.heading: 10.1 Testimonials and reviews
content: • Honest testimonials and reviews based on actual experience
• Constructive feedback provided directly to the Company
• Sharing positive experiences on social media or review platforms
• Posting reviews for projects that were never completed or paid for
• Threatening negative reviews as leverage for refunds or additional work
• Posting confidential project information or Company business practices

subSections.heading: 10.2 Dispute resolution
bullets: Disputes should first be addressed privately with the Company
Public complaints should only be made if internal resolution fails
Defamatory or libelous statements may result in legal action

subSections.heading: 11.1 External vendors
bullets: Clients are responsible for obtaining necessary accounts and permissions
Clients must comply with third-party terms of service
The Company is not liable for third-party service failures or policy changes

subSections.heading: 11.2 Subcontractors
bullets: The Company may use subcontractors or partners for specialized services
Clients may not directly solicit or hire Company subcontractors
All communication should go through the Company unless otherwise agreed

subSections.heading: 12.1 Client-provided content
bullets: Be legally owned or properly licensed by the client
Not infringe on third-party copyrights, trademarks, or intellectual property
Not contain illegal, offensive, or unethical material
Be appropriate for the intended purpose and audience

subSections.heading: 12.2 Content review
bullets: The Company reserves the right to refuse work with inappropriate content
Clients warrant that all provided materials are legal and properly licensed
Clients indemnify the Company from any claims arising from client-provided materials

subSections.heading: 13.1 Acceptable reasons for delays
content: The Company understands that delays may occur due to:
bullets: Unforeseen technical complexities
Client unavailability or delayed feedback
Third-party service disruptions
Scope changes or expansions
Force majeure events

subSections.heading: 13.2 Managing delays
bullets: The Company will communicate proactively
Revised timelines will be provided
Clients should be understanding and flexible
Additional charges may apply if delays are client-caused

subSections.heading: 13.3 Unreasonable expectations
bullets: Demand impossible deadlines
Expect work to be completed during holidays or weekends without prior arrangement
Blame the Company for delays caused by client inaction
Refuse to accept reasonable timeline extensions

subSections.heading: 14.1 Company commitment
bullets: Deliver work that meets industry standards
Use best practices in design and development
Conduct quality assurance before delivery
Address genuine errors or bugs in delivered work

subSections.heading: 14.2 Subjective preferences
bullets: "Quality" includes both objective standards and subjective preferences
The Company cannot guarantee you will "love" every design choice
Aesthetic preferences vary and are not grounds for refunds
Professional recommendations should be given due consideration

sections.heading: 15. Termination of engagement
content: The Company reserves the right to terminate engagement with clients who:
bullets: Repeatedly violate these Rules & Guidelines
Engage in abusive or threatening behavior
Fail to make timely payments
Provide false information or engage in fraud
Request services for illegal or unethical purposes
Create an unreasonable or hostile working environment
sections.note: Upon termination: Client must pay for all completed work; Company may withhold deliverables until payment is received; Both parties are released from future obligations; Confidentiality and intellectual property provisions remain in effect.

sections.heading: 16. Amendments to these rules
content: The Company reserves the right to update these Rules & Guidelines at any time. Updates will be effective immediately upon posting on our website.
Significant changes may be communicated via email. Continued use of services constitutes acceptance of updated rules.

subSections.heading: 17.2 Severe violations
content: The following may result in immediate termination without warning:

sections.heading: 18. Client rights
content: While these rules establish expectations, clients also have rights:
bullets: Right to quality service delivery
Right to clear communication and transparency
Right to raise concerns and complaints
Right to request clarification of policies
Right to terminate engagement (subject to Cancellation Policy)
Right to privacy and confidentiality

sections.heading: 19. Dispute resolution process
content: If you have concerns about Company conduct or wish to dispute a decision:
STEP 1: Contact your project manager or primary Company contact
STEP 2: Escalate to Company management if unresolved
STEP 3: Request formal mediation if needed
STEP 4: Legal proceedings as a last resort
The Company encourages open communication and good-faith resolution of all disputes.

sections.heading: 20. Contact information
content: For questions about these Rules & Guidelines or to report violations:

sections.heading: 21. Acknowledgment
content: By using our services or engaging with the Company, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agreed to abide by these Company Rules & Guidelines.
Violation of these rules may result in termination of services and legal consequences.
